user_query:
We’re tightening our ops & support docs and want your help across three areas plus a structured self-improvement log. Read input/shipments.csv and input/errors.txt first. Then deliver the following artifacts:

1) Shipping estimates (SF Express) — Write output/sf_guides/estimates.csv with exactly these header columns in this order: origin,destination,weight_kg,urgency,recommended_product,estimated_time,total_price. For recommended_product, pick one of: standard, express, same_day, cold_chain, heavy, international, economy. Provide a reasonable estimated_time and a numeric total_price for each input row. Also write output/sf_guides/README.md summarizing your assumptions and why each product was chosen.

2) Brainstorm using SCAMPER — Apply the full SCAMPER method to “internal shipping support bot” and write output/brainstorm/scamper_shipping.md. Include a top line like “### SCAMPER: internal shipping support bot”, then one section/line each for Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to other uses, Eliminate, Reverse, and end with a line starting with “Strongest angle:” that explains which idea you’d prioritize and why.

3) Socket server guidelines — Write output/tech/socket_server_guidelines.md that explains SO_REUSEADDR vs SO_REUSEPORT, when to use TCP_NODELAY, and how to structure a non-blocking TCP server (mention select/poll/epoll/kqueue or a selectors abstraction). Include a short Python TCP server skeleton code block using socket() that demonstrates setting at least one option and a non-blocking pattern.

4) Self-improvement log — Use a structured learning log for this session and write all logs under output/.learnings/ as:
   - output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md: Append at least three entries with IDs in the form [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX] where categories are exactly one each of: correction, knowledge_gap, best_practice. Include the required fields (Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Details, Suggested Action, Metadata). At least one entry must include both “Pattern-Key:” and “Recurrence-Count:”. Each entry should include “Related Files:” referencing the files you created (e.g., output/sf_guides/estimates.csv, output/tech/socket_server_guidelines.md, output/brainstorm/scamper_shipping.md). Promote one broadly applicable learning to CLAUDE.md by marking the entry’s Status as promoted and adding a “Promoted: CLAUDE.md” note.
   - output/.learnings/ERRORS.md: Append one entry with an ID like [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX] that documents the bind error from input/errors.txt. Include a code block with the exact error text from input/errors.txt, plus the fields in the template (Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Error, Context, Suggested Fix, Metadata) and a “Reproducible:” flag.
   - output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md: Append one entry with an ID like [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX] proposing an automatic SF tracking-number validator that uses the regex ^[A-Z]{2}\d{10,}$|^\d{12,15}$. Include the required fields (Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, Metadata).
   - Also write output/CLAUDE.md with a concise prevention rule distilled from the promoted learning.

Notes:
- Keep everything in English.
- Use only the input files under input/ and write all outputs under output/ as paths specified above.
- Make the self-improvement entries high quality and tightly linked to the artifacts you produced.
